title: Crash-leak scratch cleans itself + managed ignore covers the scratch shapes (D3)
action: Per CONTEXT D3. (A) scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs: cleanStaleTmpDirs (~:155-178) matches only `${base}.tmp-`; extend it to sweep `${base}.old-*` under the SAME live-pid discipline (never remove a dir owned by a live pid — a concurrent render's backup), and move the success-path `rmSync(backupRoot)` (~:208) into the `finally` that already guards the tmp dir (~:209-211) so a kill between the swap (~:201) and cleanup cannot leak it. (B) skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/fsutil.mjs writeJsonAtomic (~:87-91) and the jsonl/text atomic variants in templates/lib/decisions.mjs (~:218, ~:983): if the write-or-rename throws, unlink the tmp best-effort and RETHROW the original error unchanged. Do NOT move the tmp out of the target directory (CONTEXT D2 rationale: adjacency is what makes renameSync atomic; a cross-device rename would fail EXDEV). (C) skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs GITIGNORE_BLOCK_PATTERNS (~:102-130): add `.bee/tmp/`, `.bee/backups/`, a pattern matching ONLY the atomic-tmp shape (`<pid>-<counter>-<random>.tmp` suffix — a human's hand-made `notes.tmp` MUST stay visible to git), and the four plugin swap-dir patterns (.claude-plugin/skills.tmp-*/, .claude-plugin/skills.old-*/, .codex-plugin/skills.tmp-*/, .codex-plugin/skills.old-*/). Keep the block's existing comment style — say WHY, like the lockfile comment does. Skip any entry th-4 already added. RED-FIRST: checks first, run red, record --passed false, then implement. Checks: a leaked `<base>.old-<deadpid>-x` dir is swept by the next render while a live-pid one survives; a forced rename failure leaves no tmp and rethrows the ORIGINAL error; after onboard --apply the managed block contains the new patterns; `notes.tmp` is NOT ignored while `x.json.12345-0-abcdef.tmp` IS. Mirror fsutil/decisions to .bee/bin, render, manifest --check. TARGETED verify only. NOTE: scripts/test_render_race.mjs scenario (a) is a known WSL2 flake (backlog P2) — rerun once before reporting a failure.

truths: stale .old-* from a dead pid swept; live-pid .old-* never touched
.old-* removal sits in the finally path, not only the success path
a failed atomic rename leaves no tmp AND rethrows the original error unchanged
managed block ignores .bee/tmp/, .bee/backups/, the atomic-tmp shape and the four swap-dir patterns — while a hand-made notes.tmp stays visible
RED-FIRST failing run recorded before implementation
prohibitions: Never remove a sibling dir owned by a LIVE pid
Never swallow or replace the original error when cleaning up
Never add a greedy *.tmp ignore rule
Do not move atomic tmp files out of their target's directory
